title: SQL Learning Roadmap
description: A complete, staged path from beginner to expert — database and relational foundations, data types, querying and filtering, functions and aggregation, joins, subqueries and CTEs, set operations, DML and DDL, constraints, views, indexes, transactions, window functions, advanced querying, procedural SQL, semi-structured data, database design, performance tuning, security, administration, distributed and analytical SQL, dialects and the modern SQL ecosystem.

stages.title: Databases & Relational Foundations
stages.summary: What databases are, the relational model and the SQL language landscape.

stages.title: Data Types
stages.summary: The built-in types SQL uses to store numbers, text, dates, binary and special values.

stages.title: Querying Data
stages.summary: The SELECT statement: choosing columns, expressions and how queries are processed.

stages.title: Filtering, Operators & Predicates
stages.summary: Restricting rows with WHERE, comparison and logical operators, patterns and conditionals.

stages.title: Sorting, Limiting & Pagination
stages.summary: Ordering result sets and returning limited, paginated slices of rows.

stages.title: Built-in Functions
stages.summary: Scalar functions for strings, numbers, dates, conversion, NULLs, conditions and system info.

stages.title: Aggregation & Grouping
stages.summary: Summarizing rows with aggregate functions, GROUP BY, HAVING and grouping extensions.

stages.title: Joins & Combining Tables
stages.summary: Combining rows from multiple tables with the full range of join types and conditions.

stages.title: Subqueries & CTEs
stages.summary: Nested queries, correlated subqueries and common table expressions including recursion.

stages.title: Set Operations
stages.summary: Combining result sets vertically with UNION, INTERSECT and EXCEPT.

stages.title: Data Modification (DML)
stages.summary: Inserting, updating, deleting and merging data, and returning the affected rows.

stages.title: Data Definition (DDL) & Schema Objects
stages.summary: Creating and altering tables, schemas, databases and other structural objects.

stages.title: Constraints, Keys & Integrity
stages.summary: Enforcing data integrity with NOT NULL, UNIQUE, primary/foreign keys, CHECK and DEFAULT.

stages.title: Views & Materialized Views
stages.summary: Virtual and precomputed result sets for abstraction, security and performance.

stages.title: Indexes & Storage Structures
stages.summary: How indexes and physical storage accelerate access, and how to design them.

stages.title: Transactions, Concurrency & Locking
stages.summary: ACID transactions, isolation levels, locking, MVCC and deadlock handling.

stages.title: Window Functions & Analytic Queries
stages.summary: Computing values across row windows for ranking, offsets and running aggregates.

stages.title: Advanced Query Techniques
stages.summary: Pivoting, hierarchical queries, data generation, dynamic SQL, hints and temporal queries.

stages.title: Procedural SQL
stages.summary: Stored procedures, functions, triggers, cursors, control flow and error handling.

stages.title: Semi-Structured & Specialized Data
stages.summary: Working with JSON, XML, arrays, spatial, full-text, vector and graph data in SQL.

stages.title: Database Design & Normalization
stages.summary: Modeling data, normal forms, denormalization and schema design patterns.

stages.title: Performance Tuning & Query Optimization
stages.summary: Execution plans, indexing, query rewriting, statistics, caching and monitoring.

stages.title: Security & Access Control
stages.summary: Authentication, privileges, roles, encryption, injection defense and auditing.

stages.title: Database Administration & Operations
stages.summary: Installing, backing up, scaling, monitoring, maintaining and migrating databases.

stages.title: Scalability & Distributed SQL
stages.summary: Partitioning, sharding, replication, distributed consistency and NewSQL databases.

stages.title: Analytical SQL, OLAP & Data Warehousing
stages.summary: Analytical workloads, dimensional modeling, columnar storage and modern warehouses.

stages.title: SQL Dialects & Platforms
stages.summary: The major SQL engines, their distinctive features and how to write portable SQL.

stages.title: SQL Ecosystem, Tooling & Modern Practices
stages.summary: Application access, ORMs, migrations, pipelines, AI-assisted SQL and SQL:2023 features.
